items.title: VI Live: Ajax ontsnapt aan achterstand na buitenspel bij goal Ouaissa
items.summary: Ajax neemt het zaterdag op tegen NEC. De twee ploegen zijn dit seizoen concurrenten van elkaar in de strijd om plek twee, goed voor een direct Champions League-ticket. Het duel gaat om 21.00 uur van start en volg je in deze VI Live.

items.title: Manchester City v Newcastle: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Scott Newcastle get the ball rolling! “No-one actually believes we’ll win this game, do they?” sighs Toon fan Chris Paraskevas. “I mean the last time we won at the Etihad, the goal-scorers were Moussa Sissoko (now ruining his reputation at Panathinaikos with Rafa Benitez) and Ryan Taylor.” The teams are out. Manchester City, in sky blue, are given a guard of honour by members of their 1976 League Cup winning side. The 50th anniversary of that victory, over Newcastle, comes up next week. The Toon in third-choice blue. As for the weather, Bert Challenor, the talent scout from Comedians by Trevor Griffiths, says it best: “I’ll never understand why they don’t run boats to Manchester.” We’ll be off in a minute. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: VAR zet streep door openingstreffer NAC, Man City op 1-1
items.summary: Zaterdagavond staat er met NAC Breda tegen FC Volendam een ware degradatiekraker op het programma. De duels van PSV en Ajax volgen we in een apart liveblog. Verder staat ons een druk programma in het buitenland te wachten. Mis niets met VI Live.

items.title: Laatkomers Heerenveen schuldbewust, Veldman eist topsportcultuur
items.summary: SC Heerenveen ging zaterdag onderuit bij koploper PSV . Na afloop zal het in Friesland vooral gaan over Maxence Rivera en Vasilios Zagaritis. Het duo kwam te laat voor de wedstrijdbespreking en werd gestraft door trainer Robin Veldman.

items.title: Sources: Prestianni told UEFA he used anti-gay slur
items.summary: Gianluca Prestianni has already given evidence in UEFA's investigation into his alleged racist abuse of Vinícius Júnior, sources told ESPN.

items.title: 654 and counting: Milner becomes PL record holder
items.summary: James Milner has broken the record for the most appearances in Premier League history.

items.title: Infantino to face no IOC action over Trump event
items.summary: The International Olympic Committee will take no action against its member Gianni Infantino for a possible breach of political neutrality rules over aligning FIFA with U.S. President Donald Trump's Board of Peace.

items.title: Late winner sees Real Madrid stunned by Osasuna
items.summary: Raul García struck a dramatic stoppage-time winner as Osasuna beat LaLiga leaders Real Madrid 2-1 at Estadio El Sadar.

items.title: West Ham miss chance to boost survival hopes with wasteful draw against Bournemouth
items.summary: When it comes to the back end of May and West Ham United are reflecting on the season that was, there is every chance that this soggy Saturday will go down as a missed opportunity that proved crucial in their survival plight. Victory here would have raised the possibility of Nuno Espírito Santo’s side ending the weekend out of the relegation zone for the first time since early December. In keeping with those of recent weeks, much of the performance was befitting of another three points. But, thanks to a couple of smart Djordje Petrovic saves and some profligacy in front of goal, they had to make do with a draw – an expected goals (xG) total of 2.87 from 20 shots yielding a blank in the only column that matters. Continue reading...

items.title: Pepi en Boadu trefzeker: PSV herpakt zich na misstap in Volendam
items.summary: PSV heeft zaterdagavond een volgende stap gezet richting een hele vroege landstitel. Een week na de misstap tegen FC Volendam wonnen de Eindhovenaren in eigen huis van SC Heerenveen, dat nog wel brutaal op voorsprong kwam.

items.title: Deze omzetting van Bosz neutraliseerde Heerenveen
items.summary: PSV heeft in eigen huis sc Heerenveen verslagen: 3-1. Tactische analyse van een wedstrijd waarin PSV in de tweede helft het gevaar van Heerenveen neutraliseert.

items.title: Red cards and poor home form - Chelsea 'set fire to points'
items.summary: Chelsea have more red cards than any side in the Premier League this season, and boss Liam Rosenior says they have "set fire" to points.

items.title: 'Like landing on the moon': How Milner set a near-unbreakable Premier League record
items.summary: At age 40, James Milner is now the Premier League's all-time appearances leader. Teammates and coaches -- from Ashley Young to Jurgen Klopp -- share what makes him special.

items.title: 'Like a dream' - Abraham relishing Villa return
items.summary: Seven years since firing them into the Premier League, Tammy Abraham is back scoring goals at Aston Villa to keep their fans and players dreaming of glory.

items.title: Scotland leave it late to edge Wales in Cardiff
items.summary: Scotland left it late to deny Wales a first Six Nations victory for three years, edging in front with five minutes to play to seal a 26-23 victory in Cardiff.

items.title: Chelsea lacked ruthlessness in Burnley draw - Rosenior
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior believes his side lacked the ruthlessness in their 1-1 draw with Burnley at Stamford Bridge in the Premier League.

items.title: From £70 a week to Premier League record 24 years later
items.summary: James Milner has broken the Premier League appearance record, 24 years after making his debut as a 16-year-old.

items.title: 'Drive and hunger' take Milner to Premier League appearance record
items.summary: Brighton's James Milner starts against Brentford to break the all-time Premier League appearance record in his 654th match.

items.title: 'Not good enough' - Rosenior on Chelsea's FIVE big problems after Burnley draw
items.summary: It happened again for Chelsea - and perhaps the most damning situation of all is that you could see it coming a mile off.

items.title: Tammy Abraham salvages late point for Aston Villa to deny battling Leeds
items.summary: This felt like the day Aston Villa’s faint title hopes were extinguished despite Tammy Abraham’s late equaliser against Leeds. Unai Emery’s side have won only once in their past four Premier League home games and fell short again to leave them seven points behind leaders Arsenal. They were punished for a sloppy opening 45 minutes when they failed to get almost anything right against their more aggressive opponents. Anton Stach’s incredible free-kick looked like it would secure victory for Leeds until the 88th minute but it felt like another important step in their fight for survival. Continue reading...
